description: The immutable, append-only, queryable record of every significant action with field-level change detail (RA buildUpdateMetadata recursive humanised diff + AB append-only timestamped log that cannot be deleted). Every committed change emits one audit entry and one real-time event (axiom A13). Categorized for filtering. It serves internal-audit needs via read access rather than a separate role, and underpins the ISO 7.5 records requirements. Entries are never edited or deleted.

fields.name: auditId
fields.label: Audit Entry No.
fields.validation: system-generated; unique; immutable; monotonic append order
fields.purpose: Stable key for one audit entry; the append order preserves the chronological trail an auditor reads. Never reused, never deleted, so the trail is tamper-evident.

fields.name: category
fields.label: Category
fields.validation: one of the AuditCategory enum {TICKET | SUPPLIER | ITEM | ADMIN | PAYMENT}
fields.purpose: Classifies the entry so the audit view filters by domain (ticket vs supplier vs admin/master vs payment); the dimension an internal auditor narrows the trail by.

fields.name: actorId
fields.label: Actor
fields.validation: the user who performed the action; 'Platform/System' for automated actions (auto-approval, code generation)
fields.purpose: Records who did it (or the system for automated acts like auto-approval) so accountability and SoD verification (who approved vs who requested) are auditable; the actor an auditor traces a change to.

fields.name: action
fields.label: Action
fields.validation: non-blank; the verb/event e.g. CREATE, UPDATE, APPROVE, AUTO_APPROVE, REASSIGN, SUSPEND, IMPORT, SOD_RELAX
fields.purpose: Names what happened so the trail reads as events; a relaxed SoD rule, an auto-approval, and a suspension each record their own action verb the auditor scans for.

fields.name: entityType
fields.label: Entity Type
fields.validation: non-blank; the entity class changed e.g. Requisition, PurchaseOrder, Supplier, TaxCode
fields.purpose: The kind of record the entry concerns so the trail can be filtered to one entity class and joined with entityId to locate the exact record.

fields.name: entityId
fields.label: Entity
fields.validation: the changed record's id/identifier (e.g. requisition identifier, supplier code); with entityType locates the record
fields.purpose: Pins the entry to the exact record changed so the full history of one requisition/PO/supplier can be assembled; the join key from a record's detail screen to its audit trail.

fields.name: fieldDiffs
fields.label: Field Changes
fields.validation: array of {field, oldValue, newValue} from the recursive humanised diff (RA buildUpdateMetadata); references resolved to display values; empty for create/delete events
fields.purpose: The field-level before/after detail (humanised, references resolved to names) so an auditor sees exactly what changed, not just that something did; the substance of the change-detail requirement.

fields.name: reason
fields.label: Reason / Note
fields.validation: the reason captured with the action, e.g. budget-override reason, send-back note, SoD-relax justification
fields.purpose: Carries the mandatory reason attached to exception actions (budget override, send-back, SoD relaxation) so the trail explains WHY, the context an auditor needs to judge the change.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.label: Timestamp
fields.validation: set at append in tenant timezone; immutable; defines the chronological order of the trail
fields.purpose: When the action occurred; orders the trail and feeds cycle-time/approval-SLA analytics and the 'who did what when' reconstruction; immutable so the timeline cannot be back-dated.

invariants: A13 every committed change emits exactly one audit entry and one real-time event; access control is enforced on the guarded re-fetch, not the event stream
entries are immutable, append-only, and cannot be edited or deleted
category drawn from the AuditCategory controlled vocabulary
rejected bulk imports emit no audit entry
